name: Keyboard Operations
title: Keyboard Operations
intro: The following key combinations may be used when working in the Panel Designer:

paragraphs: In dialogs/window, help is displayed for the particular dialog/window.
tables: Opens help. In dialogs/window, help is displayed for the particular dialog/window.

sections.heading: Controlling the Cursor in the Panel’s Working Area

paragraphs: If the focus is in the panel's working area, you can press <Esc> to select the parent element of the currently selected control.
tables: If the focus is in the panel's working area, you can press <Esc> to select the parent element of the currently selected control. Example A Radio button located in a group box is selected. When <Escape> is pressed, the parent group box is selected. Several elements are placed very close to one another on a panel. It is not possible to use the mouse to select the panel itself. The solution is to continue pressing <Escape> until the panel is selected.

Example A Radio button located in a group box is selected. When <Escape> is pressed, the parent group box is selected. Several elements are placed very close to one another on a panel. It is not possible to use the mouse to select the panel itself. The solution is to continue pressing <Escape> until the panel is selected.

Example A Radio button located in a group box is selected. When <Escape> is pressed, the parent group box is selected. Several elements are placed very close to one another on a panel. It is not possible to use the mouse to select the panel itself. The solution is to continue pressing <Escape> until the panel is selected.

Example A Radio button located in a group box is selected. When <Escape> is pressed, the parent group box is selected. Several elements are placed very close to one another on a panel. It is not possible to use the mouse to select the panel itself. The solution is to continue pressing <Escape> until the panel is selected.

paragraphs: Selects the ribbon and activates the selection of the tabs.
tables: Selects the ribbon and activates the selection of the tabs. After that the following functions are available:
Opens the tab for the selected character.
Opens the menu/window for the selected character on th eselected tab.
Opens the tab to the left or right of the active tab.
Moves the selection of menu commands within the opened menu.
Executes the command of the selected menu command.
